 def parse_host_port(value: str, default_port: int = 80) -> tuple[str, int]:
+    """Parse a host[:port] string.
+
+    Supports IPv6 addresses in brackets, e.g. ``[::1]:8080``.
+    If no port is provided, ``default_port`` is used.
+    """
+    # Handle IPv6 bracketed addresses
+    if value.startswith("["):
+        # Find the closing bracket
+        closing = value.find("]")
+        if closing == -1:
+            # Malformed, fall back to default behaviour
+            return value, default_port
+        host = value[1:closing]
+        rest = value[closing + 1:]
+        if rest.startswith(":"):
+            port_text = rest[1:]
+            if port_text == "":
+                return host, default_port
+            return host, int(port_text)
+        # No port specification
+        return host, default_port
+    # Regular hostname handling
     host, sep, port_text = value.partition(":")
     if not sep:
         return value, default_port
"
